sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented for this RPG

special_mechanics.name: Party-Based Movement
special_mechanics.description: Player controls a four-member party moving freely across a 3D isometric world. Movement is controlled via directional pad (SNES version) rather than mouse cursor (PC versions). Maneuvering around scenery is noted as clunkier on SNES compared to computer versions.

special_mechanics.name: Real-Time Tactics Combat
special_mechanics.description: Party can be attacked while stationary. Player can activate defense magic, move around, and switch weapons during battles. Some enemies move at speeds faster than the player party can keep up with, including enemies that zigzag sporadically.

special_mechanics.name: Enemy Movement Variation
special_mechanics.description: Over 100 different enemy variants with distinct movement and attack patterns. Some enemies (e.g., certain foes) move at significantly greater speeds than the player party, making them difficult to evade or pursue.

tier_notes: Tier 3 empirical/qualitative data. Drakkhen is an RPG with party-based movement rather than character physics-driven mechanics. No precise jump physics, gravity values, or sub-pixel systems are documented. Movement parameters are qualitatively described as clunkier on SNES vs PC versions due to interface differences.
sources_used: Wikipedia - Drakkhen article (gameplay mechanics, movement descriptions, SNES vs PC differences)
